suppressedMessages.message: Unexpected any. Specify a different type.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.
Use `never` instead, this is useful when instantiating generic type parameters that you don't need to know the type of.

suggestions.desc: Use `unknown` instead, this will force you to explicitly, and safely assert the type is correct.

suppressedMessages.message:
Error: Calling setState synchronously within an effect can trigger cascading renders

Effects are intended to synchronize state between React and external systems such as manually updating the DOM, state management libraries, or other platform APIs. In general, the body of an effect should do one or both of the following:
* Update external systems with the latest state from React.
* Subscribe for updates from some external system, calling setState in a callback function when external state changes.

Calling setState synchronously within an effect body causes cascading renders that can hurt performance, and is not recommended. (https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect).

C:\Users\Yeasin arafat shuvo\Documents\New Street Food\app\map\page.tsx:128:23
  126 |   useEffect(() => {
  127 |     // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/set-state-in-effect
> 128 |     if (userLocation) loadStalls();
      |                       ^^^^^^^^^^ Avoid calling setState() directly within an effect
  129 |   }, [userLocation]); // eslint-disable-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
  130 |
  131 |   const handleMapMove = useCallback((bounds: { minLat: number; minLng: number; maxLat: number; maxLng: number }) => {
React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'loadStalls'. Either include it or remove the dependency array.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [loadStalls, userLocation]

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies: 'onMapMove', 'onUserLocationFound', and 'theme'. Either include them or remove the dependency array. If 'onMapMove' changes too often, find the parent component that defines it and wrap that definition in useCallback.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [onMapMove, onUserLocationFound, theme]

suppressedMessages.message: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'onStallSelect'. Either include it or remove the dependency array. If 'onStallSelect' changes too often, find the parent component that defines it and wrap that definition in useCallback.

suggestions.desc: Update the dependencies array to be: [stalls, selectedStallId, mapReady, onStallSelect]
